rules.finding_pattern: Widened mediastinum on chest radiograph in a patient with acute chest pain
rules.why_it_matters: Classic (though insensitive) sign of thoracic aortic pathology. Must be excluded before proceeding further down the ACS-directed pathway — anticoagulation or thrombolysis in an undiagnosed dissection is frequently fatal.

rules.finding_pattern: CT angiography demonstrating an intimal flap involving the ascending aorta
rules.why_it_matters: Confirms Stanford Type A (DeBakey I) dissection — a surgical emergency. Untreated mortality rises roughly 1-2% per hour from symptom onset.

recommendations.text: Widened mediastinum on CXR in a patient with acute chest pain warrants immediate CT angiography of the chest BEFORE further ACS-directed anticoagulation, cath activation, or thrombolysis.
recommendations.rationale: Missing an aortic dissection on the ACS pathway is a well-documented cause of preventable death — anticoagulation or thrombolysis in a patient with dissection dramatically worsens outcomes and complicates emergent surgical repair.
recommendations.evidence_line: AATS/ACC/AHA 2022 Guideline — screen for dissection before ACS-directed therapy when mediastinal contour is abnormal

recommendations.text: Stanford Type A dissection confirmed. Emergently consult cardiovascular surgery — do not wait for troponin or further cardiology workup.
recommendations.rationale: Type A dissection is a surgical emergency; every hour of delay measurably increases mortality.
recommendations.evidence_line: AATS/ACC/AHA 2022 Guideline — emergent surgical consultation for Type A dissection

actions.label: Update CV Surgery on-call

recommendations.text: HOLD apixaban and aspirin. Do not pursue cath, thrombolysis, or further anticoagulation for the presumed ACS picture — active anticoagulation in a dissection sharply raises rupture and surgical bleeding risk.
recommendations.rationale: The ACS-directed plan from 11:00 is now superseded; continuing anticoagulation works directly against emergent surgical repair.
recommendations.evidence_line: AATS/ACC/AHA 2022 Guideline — anticoagulation contraindicated once dissection is confirmed

orderDetails.indication: Confirmed Stanford Type A dissection — anticoagulation/antiplatelet contraindicated pending surgical repair

recommendations.text: Aggressive heart rate and blood pressure control: IV beta-blocker first (target HR <60, SBP 100-120), then a vasodilator if BP remains elevated after adequate rate control.
recommendations.rationale: Reducing dP/dt (rate of aortic wall stress) lowers propagation risk while awaiting the OR; beta-blockade must precede vasodilators to avoid reflex tachycardia worsening shear stress.
recommendations.evidence_line: AATS/ACC/AHA 2022 Guideline — impulse control (rate before pressure) in acute dissection

recommendations.text: Type & crossmatch — anticipate need for blood products with emergent aortic repair.
recommendations.rationale: Type A repair carries high transfusion likelihood; blood bank turnaround should not be the rate-limiting step once the OR is ready.
recommendations.evidence_line: Institutional massive transfusion protocol — proactive activation for anticipated emergent cardiac surgery

orderDetails.indication: Anticipated emergent Type A aortic dissection repair

recommendations.text: Book the OR emergently. Pre-fill the case request with diagnosis, indication, anticipated blood products, and cannulation preferences so CV surgery and anesthesia can move the moment they arrive.
recommendations.rationale: Every step pre-staged before the surgical team arrives is time saved on the mortality clock.
recommendations.evidence_line: AATS/ACC/AHA 2022 Guideline — minimize time-to-OR for Type A dissection

items: Procedure: Emergent ascending aorta ± arch replacement
Diagnosis: Stanford Type A (DeBakey I) acute aortic dissection
Blood products: 4 units PRBC, 2 units FFP on standby (type & cross in progress)
Cannulation preference: right axillary artery (arch involvement present) — per CV surgery to confirm
orderDetails.indication: CT-confirmed Stanford Type A dissection, ascending aorta 4.7 cm at sinotubular junction

recommendations.text: Establish two large-bore IVs and an arterial line for continuous hemodynamic monitoring. Defer right radial/brachial access pending CV surgery's cannulation plan.
recommendations.rationale: Right-arm access can conflict with axillary cannulation planning; left-sided or femoral access avoids pre-empting the surgical approach.
recommendations.evidence_line: Institutional practice — avoid instrumenting the planned surgical cannulation site

recommendations.text: Serial neurologic and pulse checks (all four extremities) while awaiting the OR — watch for malperfusion syndrome (stroke, mesenteric ischemia, limb ischemia, renal involvement) as the dissection extends into the descending aorta.
recommendations.rationale: Malperfusion changes the urgency and surgical approach and can develop rapidly; frequent reassessment catches it early.
recommendations.evidence_line: AATS/ACC/AHA 2022 Guideline — malperfusion surveillance in acute dissection
